source: San Diego Housing Commission, CalHFA, GSFA, DCCA, SD Black Homebuyers Program

programs.name: SDHC Low-Income Down Payment & Closing Cost Assistance
programs.maxAssistanceDescription: Up to 22% of purchase price as a deferred loan + $10,000 closing cost grant

incomeLimit.description: Household income at or below 80% of Area Median Income

programs.notes: Deferred second mortgage with 0% interest. Repayment due upon sale, transfer, refinance, or payoff of first mortgage. Must occupy as primary residence. Property must be located within the City of San Diego limits.

programs.name: SDHC Middle-Income Down Payment Assistance

incomeLimit.description: Household income between 80% and 150% of Area Median Income

programs.notes: Deferred second mortgage repayable upon sale, transfer, refinance, or payoff. $40K down payment loan plus $10K closing cost grant. Must be primary residence within City of San Diego limits.

programs.name: County of San Diego DCCA/CalHome Program
programs.agency: San Diego County Department of Community & Consumer Affairs (DCCA)
programs.maxAssistanceDescription: Up to 17% of purchase price as deferred loan + $10,000 closing cost assistance

incomeLimit.description: Household income at or below 80% of Area Median Income

programs.notes: Available in unincorporated San Diego County and participating cities OUTSIDE the City of San Diego. Deferred loan with shared appreciation. CalHome funding — availability depends on state allocation cycles.

programs.name: CalHFA MyHome Assistance Program
programs.maxAssistanceDescription: Up to 3.5% of purchase price (FHA) or 3% (conventional) as a deferred junior loan

incomeLimit.description: CalHFA income limits — varies by county and household size, generally moderate income

programs.notes: Must use a CalHFA-approved lender. Deferred payment, simple interest loan. 3.5% for FHA loans, 3% for conventional loans. Can be combined with other CalHFA programs. Borrower must complete homebuyer education.

programs.name: CalHFA Dream For All Shared Appreciation Loan
programs.maxAssistanceDescription: Up to 20% of purchase price (max $150,000) as a shared appreciation loan

incomeLimit.description: Income limits vary by county — generally targets first-generation homebuyers and low-to-moderate income households

programs.notes: Extremely competitive — allocated via lottery when funding becomes available. Shared appreciation: borrower repays the original loan amount PLUS a proportional share of any home value increase. First-generation homebuyers given priority. Check CalHFA website for next funding round.

programs.name: GSFA Platinum Down Payment Assistance
programs.maxAssistanceDescription: Up to 5.5% of first mortgage loan amount as a gift or second lien

incomeLimit.description: Low-to-moderate income — limits vary by county and household size

programs.notes: Does NOT require first-time buyer status — repeat buyers eligible. Assistance may be structured as a non-repayable gift or a repayable second lien depending on the program option selected. Must use a GSFA-approved lender. Widely available and less restrictive than government programs.

programs.name: San Diego Black Homebuyers Program
programs.agency: San Diego Foundation / Black Homebuyers Program Partners
programs.maxAssistanceDescription: Up to $40,000 in down payment and closing cost assistance

incomeLimit.description: Household income at or below 120% of Area Median Income

programs.notes: Targeted program to address the homeownership gap for Black/African American homebuyers in San Diego County. Must complete homebuyer education. Check program website for current funding availability and application periods.
